meta.description: High-signal items only: contracts, earnings, funding, IPO signals, defense awards, acquisitions. urgency: immediate=act now, same_day=review today, watch=monitor

immediate.title: Transporter-17 Sends the First Commercial Nuclear Sat to Orbit

immediate.title: Isar Aerospace signs agreement for Canadian launch site
immediate.summary: Isar Aerospace has signed a contract to develop a Canadian launch site for its Spectrum rocket, an agreement linked to a deal to sell submarines to the Canadian military. The post Isar Aerospace signs agreement for Canadian launch site appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: LRASM Clears Integration Milestone for Use on the B-1B
immediate.summary: The Air Force recently tested the Long-Range Anti-Ship Missile on the B-1B Lancer, clearing a key milestone for integrating the weapon on its bombers. The post LRASM Clears Integration Milestone for Use on the B-1B appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Iridium folds Aireon aviation safety service into Rocket Lab-bound business
immediate.summary: Iridium Communications has completed its takeover of Aireon, bringing the aircraft-tracking venture fully in-house ahead of the satellite operator’s planned $8 billion sale to Rocket Lab. The post Iridium folds Aireon aviation safety service into Rocket Lab-bound business appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: ACC Battle Lab Wants More Counter-Drone Options
immediate.summary: The Air Force Point Defense Battle Lab is searching for more defense firms to demonstrate their counter-drone tech at a series of upcoming exercises aimed at focusing on the needs of Airmen defending bases from unmanned aerial attack. The post ACC Battle Lab Wants More Counter-Drone Options appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: GAO: T-7 Delays Push Full-rate Production Decision Back Two Years
immediate.summary: Multiple delays have pushed the full-rate production decision on the T-7A Red Hawk back two years, the Government Accountability Office said in a new report. The assessment is yet another setback for the Air Force's next-generation trainer aircraft, which has been plagued with issues. The post GAO: T-7 Delays Push Full-rate Production Decision Back Two Years appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Apolink makes contact with first relay satellite
immediate.summary: Apolink has made contact with its first satellite after launching on SpaceX’s July 7 rideshare mission, clearing the way for a data relay demonstration using a novel experimental license from the Federal Communications Commission. The post Apolink makes contact with first relay satellite appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: Artemis 2's Jeremy Hansen stepping down from active astronaut duty after epic moon mission
immediate.summary: The Canadian Space Agency's Jeremy Hansen will step back from active astronaut service in September for a new role.

immediate.title: NASA Transfers ‘Hundred Acre Wood’ to Patuxent Research Refuge
immediate.summary: NASA ceremonially transferred ownership of about 105 acres of wooded land at its Goddard Space Flight Center’s Greenbelt, Maryland, campus Tuesday to the adjoining Patuxent Research Refuge, managed by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service.
